heading: Inspection of carcasses, meat of which is intended for export
label: 615 Inspection of carcasses, meat of which is intended for export
text: The Secretary shall also cause to be made a careful inspection of the carcasses and parts thereof of all amenable species, the meat of which, fresh, salted, canned, corned, packed, cured, or otherwise prepared, is intended and offered for export to any foreign country, at such times and places and in such manner as he may deem proper.
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to .section 83 of this title

Amendments2005— substituted “amenable species” for “cattle, sheep, swine, goats, horses, mules, and other equines”.Pub. L. 109–971967—, §§ 3(b), 12(a), struck out “of Agriculture” after “Secretary” and included horses, mules, and other equines in the list of animals, respectively.Pub. L. 90–201

Effective Date of 1967 AmendmentAmendment by effective , except that with respect to equines (other than horses) and their carcasses and parts thereof, meat, and meat food products thereof, amendment effective upon expiration of sixty days after , see , set out as an Effective Date note under .Pub.
